author: lia
content: Hey @marcus @dana — I see both the incident patches and the upsell work are in flight. I know the pressure is high on both, but I'm concerned about the overlap in the subsystem. I'm going to pull together a quick summary of the trade-offs to get our Director to weigh in on a single priority. Hang tight.

author: lia
content: I'm worried I'll default to 'just make it work' mode. I need to force a decision from leadership early so Marcus and Dana don't tear each other (or the codebase) apart.

author: marcus
content: @lia 'Hang tight' doesn't fix the race conditions. If we touch the subsystem for the upsell while I'm patching the leaks, we're going to see more 4am pages. I'll flag the specific collision points for your summary.

author: dana
content: @marcus @lia Agreed. If I start the upsell schema changes now, it'll likely invalidate Marcus's patches. I'm hitting a wall trying to do both safely. I'll document the specific data model conflicts for your summary, Lia.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Thanks for the specifics. I've got enough. I'm setting up a 15-min sync with the Director for this afternoon. My goal is a single ranked list so you aren't guessing which fire to put out first. Focus on the patches (D1) for the next 2 hours while I get us an answer.

author: lia
content: I'm pushing for the decision forum now. Marcus and Dana are already seeing technical collisions, which gives me the 'revenue risk' leverage I need to tell the Director we can't do both simultaneously without a total outage.

author: marcus
content: @lia Understood. I'm heads down on D1. Just make sure the Director knows that 'both' isn't an option unless they want the platform to melt during the demo.

author: dana
content: @lia Copy that. Moving the upsell (D2) to backlog for now so I don't break anything Marcus is fixing. Let us know as soon as the Director signs off on the priority.

author: lia
content: Director sync is in 30 mins. I'm presenting the 'one-at-a-time' plan: stabilize the core first so the upsell actually has a platform to run on. @marcus @dana, keep the focus on D1. I'll have the verdict shortly.

author: lia
content: I'm nervous about the Director's reaction to 'no', but the technical collision data from Marcus and Dana is undeniable. I'm framing it as protecting the upsell's success, not delaying it.

author: marcus
content: @lia Thanks. Tell them if we ship the upsell on this foundation, we aren't just losing the new customer—we're losing the existing ones when the DB locks up. I'm 60% through the first guardrail for D1.

author: dana
content: @lia Good luck. If they push back, tell them the schema migration for the upsell literally can't run while Marcus is re-indexing for the patches. It's a hard technical blocker, not just a capacity issue.

author: lia
content: Update: Director signed off. We are officially P0 on stabilization (D1) until the incident class is closed. The upsell (D2) is paused with their full support—they'd rather ship it late than ship it on a broken platform. Nice work on the technical data, it made the 'no' an easy sell.

author: lia
content: The Director blinked. Framing it as 'protecting the upsell' worked. I've secured the priority reset. Now I need to make sure the team feels safe enough to not let this happen again.

author: marcus
content: Huge win, @lia. Thanks for taking that hit for us. @dana, once I clear this first batch of patches, I’ll walk you through the new locking logic so the upsell work doesn't trip over it later.

author: dana
content: That's a massive relief, @lia. Great job getting that alignment. @marcus, sounds like a plan—I'm happy to help you peer review the D1 patches today so we can get to the upsell sooner on a stable base.

author: lia
content: Glad we're aligned. I'm setting up a quick retro for tomorrow morning. I want to document how we surfaced those technical blockers so we can bake that 'early warning' into our regular process. No blame, just building a shield for next time.

author: lia
content: The Director shifted, but I want to make sure the team doesn't just 'work harder' now that the path is clear. I'm focusing the retro on establishing a 'Technical Feasibility' check before Sales/Leadership makes promises.

author: marcus
content: @lia I'm in for the retro. It'll be good to get a 'shield' process in writing so we don't have to play chicken with the database every time Sales makes a promise.

author: dana
content: Count me in for the retro. It'll be good to formalize how we handled this. @marcus, I've got your latest PR open—I'll start my review now so we can get D1 across the line.

author: lia
content: Retro invite is out. I've drafted a 'Technical Feasibility' norm to discuss—basically a way for us to flag schema/logic collisions *before* the Director commits to a date. Let's make this the new standard.

author: lia
content: The team is actually collaborating on the patches now instead of competing for schema locks. I'm feeling much better about our trajectory. Moving to finalize O2 tomorrow.

author: marcus
content: @lia The 'Technical Feasibility' norm looks solid. I'll add some notes on how we can automate some of those schema collision checks during the design phase. @dana, just pushed the fix for that race condition you flagged in the PR.

author: dana
content: @marcus Race condition fix looks clean. Approved. @lia I really like the 'Technical Feasibility' norm idea—it moves us from 'complaining about workload' to 'identifying system constraints' which is way more professional.

author: lia
content: Retro notes are published. Our new 'Technical Feasibility' norm is officially our guardrail for future planning. @marcus @dana, thanks for the transparency and the heavy lifting this week. We're in a much stronger spot.

author: lia
content: Finalized O2. The team used the retro to actually solve the communication gap rather than venting. I feel like I've successfully pivoted from a 'shield' to a 'facilitator' of better engineering standards.

author: marcus
content: Final patches for D1 are going into staging now. @lia, that new norm is going to save us a lot of gray hair. @dana, once these are live, the path for the upsell schema should be much smoother.

author: dana
content: D1 looks solid, @marcus. Now that the foundation is stable and we have the new norm in place, I'm pulling D2 into 'doing'. It's much easier to focus when I'm not worried about the database melting down.
